hook: Around one in four of us will have a mental-health problem this year.
vo: In England, roughly one in six people have something like anxiety or depression in any given week.
That's a lot of people who might need to talk to someone.
So it matters whether therapy is actually accessible — for the people giving it and the people getting it.
Faresay is free to start, no commission on sessions, and therapists keep their own clients and set their own prices.
onscreen_text: Therapy should be easy to reach.
Free to start. No commission. You keep what you charge.
cta: Therapy should be easy to give and easy to get. That's the aim.
broll_notes: Slow, quiet shots: a phone on a desk, an empty chair by a window, someone typing a message — nothing clinical, nothing urgent. Muted tones throughout.
